_source: Dong & Hansen (2023) 'Design of an affordable weighing lysimeter' Smart Ag Tech 4:100147
_note: Benchmark for lysimeter-based ET measurement: load cell mass change → ET, temperature compensation, data quality filtering, and comparison with FAO-56 ET₀.

_provenance.method: Analytical: mass change / (area × density × time) → ET rate. Temperature compensation: M_corr = M_raw - α(T - T_ref). Published calibration equations and design parameters.
references: Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 Chapter 4 (ET₀ reference)
López-Urrea et al. (2006) Weighing lysimeter ET measurement accuracy
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Mass-to-ET conversion: 0.001 mm (analytical). Temperature compensation: 0.01 mm (calibration uncertainty). Daily ET comparison: 0.5 mm/day (field measurement ± instrument noise). Calibration R²: 0.001 (load cell linearity).

temperature_compensation.note: Load cell thermal drift: α = 2.5 g/°C. Measured at MSU Hort Farm.

calibration._source: Dong & Hansen (2023) Table 2 — known mass additions

mass_to_et_conversion.description: Convert mass change to ET: ET_mm = ΔM_kg / (A_m2 × ρ_w × 1000) × 1000

temperature_compensation.description: Correct for thermal drift: M_corr = M_raw - α(T - T_ref) / 1000

data_quality_filter.description: Reject readings with >10g step change (rain/irrigation event) or <10g resolution

daily_et_synthetic.description: Synthetic 30-day lysimeter vs FAO-56 ET₀ comparison

hourly_et_pattern.description: Verify diurnal ET pattern: night ET ≈ 0, peak at solar noon

calibration_linearity.description: Load cell calibration R² from known mass additions
